key_highlights: Dow closes at record high, best first half since 2021, but tech selloff deepens across Asia and US.
Chip stocks rally in US (Lam Research, Sandisk, Ambarella hit highs) but slump in Asia on AI jitters.
Fed expected to hold rates for rest of 2026; inflation risks declining per Fed chair.
Oil tumbles most in years, boosting consumer stocks and easing inflation fears.
Trump won't renew USMCA, toppling global trade stability; also bought stocks before tariff pause.
Gold extends gains on rate-hike prospects easing; yen climbs on intervention risks.
Small-cap stocks crushing S&P 500; active ETFs recommended to play rally.
Nike earnings due; Salesforce growth story ignored; Strategy's unrealized Bitcoin losses hit $14B.
India ramps up share sales to cushion budget hit by oil; textile stocks shine on trade deals.
AI race heats up: Anthropic's Fable 5 ban lifted, Claude Science released, Amazon launches $1B FDE org.
Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky; Paramount/Warner Bros deal faces UK intervention.
Japan retail investors bought record stocks during selloff; Korea seeks finance upgrade but stocks tumble 8%.
BC Hydro changes solar power payments; egg producers settle price-fixing for $3.3M and 53M eggs.
Trump's financial disclosure lists $1.4B in crypto earnings from meme coins.
Congo plans first stock market amid AI mineral boom; Adani signs $11.5B India aluminum MOU.

sectors.trend: Divergent: US chip stocks rally to record highs (Lam Research, Sandisk, Ambarella) while Asian chip stocks tumble 8% on AI jitters. AI model releases (Anthropic Fable 5, Claude Science) and Amazon's $1B FDE org signal continued investment.
sectors.implications: Opportunity in US chip equipment and AI infrastructure names; caution on overvalued AI plays. Asian selloff may be temporary but warrants monitoring.

sectors.trend: American Airlines launches grab-and-go lounge at JFK; Tesla falls on copper price concerns; Nike earnings due; July 4 sales promotions active. 'Funflation' driving live event prices higher.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending resilient but selective. Travel and experiential spending strong; auto sector faces input cost pressure. Nike earnings key for retail sentiment.

sectors.trend: Oil tumbles most in years; China private refiners snap up Middle East oil; Iran's floating oil hoard swells; palm oil dips on weaker crude. India saves on crude imports via EV adoption.
sectors.implications: Lower oil is net positive for consumers and importers (India, Japan). Energy sector under pressure but pipeline projects (South Bow/Bridger) offer infrastructure plays.

sectors.trend: Fed expected to hold rates for rest of 2026; ECB views splinter on next move; Japan bonds fall on inflation concerns; Wall Street firms bolster Gulf teams. Q2 CLO issuance slowest since 2023.
sectors.implications: Rate-sensitive sectors (REITs, utilities) may benefit from stable rates. CLO market slowdown signals caution in credit. Gulf expansion reflects shifting capital flows.

sectors.trend: India ramps up share sales to cushion budget hit by oil; textile stocks stand out on trade deals; Adani signs $11.5B aluminum MOU; food watchdog targets energy drinks. Japan retail investors buy record stocks; Korea seeks finance upgrade but stocks tumble.
sectors.implications: India remains a bright spot with structural reforms and trade deals. Korea's AI jitters create buying opportunity for long-term investors. Japan retail buying signals confidence.

sectors.trend: West Pharmaceutical surges 6.7% on 21% revenue growth; FDA allows ZYN pouches on health benefits; pharmaceutical giant pours $11B on acquisition.
sectors.implications: Drug delivery and specialty pharma showing strong demand. M&A activity signals consolidation. ZYN approval opens new market for nicotine alternatives.

key_themes: AI arms race intensifies with model releases and massive investments
Divergent global chip market: US rallies, Asia slumps
Oil price collapse benefits consumers but pressures energy sector
Central bank rate stability expected through 2026
Trade policy uncertainty: USMCA non-renewal, India trade deals
Record retail investor activity in Japan and India
Crypto volatility: Bitcoin losses for Strategy, Trump's meme coin earnings
Infrastructure and pipeline development in energy and AI data centers
Consumer resilience amid 'funflation' and selective spending

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into small-caps, value stocks, and active ETFs. Reduce exposure to high-flying AI names with no earnings.
Reduce exposure to North American auto and agriculture sectors. Increase positions in domestic-focused companies and non-US markets.
Hold gold and yen as hedges. Monitor oil supply disruptions. Avoid direct exposure to Korean tech until volatility subsides.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Crypto Market Contagion (Bitcoin losses, meme coin volatility)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Limit crypto exposure to regulated ETFs. Avoid companies with large unrealized crypto losses (e.g., Strategy).
Favor short-duration bonds and TIPS. Invest in companies with pricing power (e.g., West Pharmaceutical, J&J Snack Foods).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory & Legal Risks (Price-fixing, AI bans, food misbranding)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid stocks facing active litigation (egg producers, memory makers). Monitor FDA decisions on AI and nicotine products.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy small-cap and mid-cap ETFs to capture rotation from large-cap tech
opportunities.rationale: Small-cap stocks are crushing the S&P 500; active ETFs offer targeted exposure. Low oil and stable rates favor domestic cyclicals.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate US semiconductor equipment stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Lam Research, Sandisk, and Ambarella hit record highs on AI demand. Asian selloff is temporary; structural growth intact.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in India-focused ETFs and textile stocks
opportunities.rationale: India's share sale plan, trade deals, and EV adoption drive growth. Textile stocks are market standouts. Adani's aluminum MOU signals infrastructure boom.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and yen as hedges against geopolitical and trade risks
opportunities.rationale: Gold extends gains on rate-hike prospects easing; yen climbs on intervention risks. USMCA non-renewal and Iran tensions support safe havens.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in consumer staples and healthcare
opportunities.rationale: West Pharmaceutical's revenue growth and J&J Snack Foods' undervaluation highlight defensive strength. Stable rates support dividend payers.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to Korean and Asian tech stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Korean stocks tumbled 8% on AI jitters; Asian tech selloff intensifying. Wait for stabilization before re-entering.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against USMCA non-renewal with short US auto and agri ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Trump's decision topples trade stability; auto and agriculture sectors face tariff risks. Short-term downside likely.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid companies with large unrealized crypto losses
defensive_moves.rationale: Strategy's $14B Bitcoin loss highlights risk. Meme coin volatility adds uncertainty. Stick to regulated crypto ETFs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Shift from long-duration bonds to short-term bond ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Japanese bonds fell on inflation concerns; Fed hold may not prevent curve steepening. Short-term bonds offer better value and lower risk.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile with a rotation from large-cap tech to small-caps and value. Oil's decline supports consumer discretionary and airlines. Trade policy uncertainty (USMCA, Iran) and AI jitters in Asia will keep investors cautious. Fed hold and declining inflation provide a floor for equities. Gold and yen will benefit from safe-haven demand.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Structural growth in AI and semiconductor equipment remains intact despite periodic selloffs. India and emerging markets offer diversification and growth. USMCA non-renewal could reshape North American trade, creating winners (domestic manufacturing) and losers (auto, agriculture). Central bank rate stability supports bond and equity valuations. Crypto remains speculative but regulated products gain traction.
key_catalysts: US payrolls data (July 3) – will confirm labor market strength
Nike earnings (July 2 after bell) – consumer spending bellwether
ECB and Fed rate decisions – any hawkish surprise could roil markets
USMCA renegotiation details – impact on auto and agriculture
AI model releases (Anthropic, Amazon) – drive tech sentiment
Oil price trajectory – affects inflation and consumer spending
India budget and share sale plan – fiscal health indicator
Korea and Japan intervention in FX markets – yen volatility
Congo stock market launch – new frontier for mineral investors
Trump legal and financial disclosures – political risk
watch_list: Semiconductor index (SOX) vs Asian chip stocks
WTI crude oil price and OPEC+ actions
Bitcoin and MSTR stock price correlation
US 10-year Treasury yield and inflation breakevens
FDA decisions on AI models and nicotine products
USMCA negotiation timeline and tariff announcements
